title: Cupania brachyphylla
intro: Cupania brachyphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cupania brachyphylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Cupania brachyphylla is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat coughs and colds, or as a tincture to alleviate fever and joint pain.
taxonomy: Cupania brachyphylla is classified within the family Sapindaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry or soapbark family. This family comprises over 1,500 species, including trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Cupania is a group of small to medium-sized trees native to Central and South America. Cupania brachyphylla is specifically found in the tropical regions of Central America. It is an evergreen species, characterized by its small leaves and white flowers.
uses: Cupania brachyphylla has been utilized in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves are reportedly used to treat fever and respiratory issues.
